title: Prove nullifier uniqueness across all three circuits

artifacts.description: Symbolic analysis document: defines nullifier construction for each circuit (HumanUniqueness, AgentPolicy, Delegation), enumerates Poseidon2 input vectors and arities, proves domain separation via distinct input tuples, and provides a collision impossibility argument.
HumanUniqueness circuit with explicit domain tag constant (DOMAIN_HUMAN = 1) prepended to Poseidon2 inputs: Poseidon2([DOMAIN_HUMAN, scope, secret]).
AgentPolicy circuit with explicit domain tag constant (DOMAIN_AGENT = 2) prepended to Poseidon2 inputs: Poseidon2([DOMAIN_AGENT, credentialField0, credentialField1, nonce]).
Delegation circuit with explicit domain tag constant (DOMAIN_DELEG = 3) prepended to Poseidon2 inputs: Poseidon2([DOMAIN_DELEG, delegatorId, delegateeId, nonce]).

Halo2 MockProver test suite: exercises cross-circuit nullifier values on shared raw inputs (same scope/secret/nonce values), asserts no two circuits produce equal nullifierHash outputs, and verifies constraint satisfaction per circuit.

Python symbolic analysis script using sympy or sage: enumerates all pairwise (circuitA, circuitB) input arity differences and domain tag differences, asserts collision is impossible under Poseidon2 preimage resistance, outputs proof table.
Formal writeup: definitions, collision resistance assumption, domain separation theorem with proof sketch, and cross-reference to test results.

steps: Audit each circuit's current nullifier construction: record exact Poseidon2 input vector length and field semantics for HumanUniqueness (scope, secret), AgentPolicy (credential fields + nonce), and Delegation (delegatorId, delegateeId, nonce).
Define DOMAIN_HUMAN=1, DOMAIN_AGENT=2, DOMAIN_DELEG=3 as field constants and prepend each to the respective Poseidon2 input vector, updating circuit constraint logic accordingly.
Update HumanUniqueness circuit: change nullifier to Poseidon2([DOMAIN_HUMAN, scope, secret]) with arity 3; add constraint asserting domain tag equals DOMAIN_HUMAN.
Update AgentPolicy circuit: change nullifier to Poseidon2([DOMAIN_AGENT, credentialField0, credentialField1, nonce]) with arity 4; add constraint asserting domain tag equals DOMAIN_AGENT.
Update Delegation circuit: change delegationNullifier to Poseidon2([DOMAIN_DELEG, delegatorId, delegateeId, nonce]) with arity 4; add constraint asserting domain tag equals DOMAIN_DELEG.

Write symbolic analysis in Python/Sage: for each pair (A, B) show input tuples differ in at least one position (domain tag or arity) making equal outputs a preimage collision under Poseidon2, which is computationally infeasible.

Implement Halo2 MockProver tests: create shared raw inputs (same numeric values for scope/credentialField0/delegatorId etc.), run all three circuits, assert pairwise nullifier inequality, assert each circuit's constraints are satisfied.

Write MockProver negative test: attempt to construct a witness for circuit B using a nullifier value produced by circuit A on the same raw input; verify the circuit rejects it (unsatisfied domain tag constraint).
Document formal theorem in nullifier_formal_proof.md: state collision resistance assumption, define domain separation as distinct Poseidon2 preimages, prove no valid cross-circuit witness exists.
Run all tests and symbolic analysis, capture output tables, embed results in docs/nullifier_domain_separation.md.
